name: Judy Tenuta
blurb: She rose to national prominence in the 1980s through HBO specials and club appearances, blending insult comedy, observational humor, and bawdy theatrics.
bio: Judy Tenuta was an American comedian, actress, and accordion-playing comedy musician known for her brash, absurdist stage persona as the self-styled Love Goddess. She rose to national prominence in the 1980s through HBO specials and club appearances, blending insult comedy, observational humor, and bawdy theatrics. She received two Grammy nominations for Best Comedy Album.

timeline.ev: Television debut on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson
Breakout in HBO special Women of the Night
Won Best Female Stand-Up at the American Comedy Awards
Died of ovarian cancer in Los Angeles

accolades: Best Female Stand-Up Comic, American Comedy Awards (1988)
Two Grammy nominations for Best Comedy Album
